title: Unable to run postgres image rootless
body:
/kind bug

### Version

1.2.0 and 1.3.0-dev at least

### How to reproduce

```
podman run -it postgres:9.6.9
```

Result:

```
The files belonging to this database system will be owned by user "postgres".
This user must also own the server process.

The database cluster will be initialized with locale "en_US.utf8".
The default database encoding has accordingly been set to "UTF8".
The default text search configuration will be set to "english".

Data page checksums are disabled.

fixing permissions on existing directory /var/lib/postgresql/data ... ok
creating subdirectories ... ok
selecting default max_connections ... 100
selecting default shared_buffers ... 128MB
selecting dynamic shared memory implementation ... posix
creating configuration files ...  	ok
running bootstrap script ... ok
performing post-bootstrap initialization ... ok
syncing data to disk ... ok

WARNING: enabling "trust" authentication for local connections
You can change this by editing pg_hba.conf or using the option -A, or
--auth-local and --auth-host, the next time you run initdb.

Success. You can now start the database server using:

    pg_ctl -D /var/lib/postgresql/data -l logfile start

****************************************************
WARNING: No password has been set for the database.
         This will allow anyone with access to the
         Postgres port to access your database. In
         Docker's default configuration, this is
         effectively any other container on the same
         system.

         Use "-e POSTGRES_PASSWORD=password" to set
         it in "docker run".
****************************************************
waiting for server to start....LOG:  could not bind Unix socket: Permission denied
HINT:  Is another postmaster already running on port 5432? If not, remove socket file "/var/run/postgresql/.s.PGSQL.5432" and retry.
WARNING:  could not create Unix-domain socket in directory "/var/run/postgresql"
FATAL:  could not create any Unix-domain sockets
LOG:  database system is shut down
 stopped waiting
pg_ctl: could not start server
Examine the log output.
```

No clue which permissions it needs or if that's selinux issue or anythin else. Also tried to set POSTGRES_PASSWORD of course.

comments.body: What OS? We were seeing SELinux issues creating unix sockets in containers recently on Fedora.
Fedora 29, Linux linux.fritz.box 5.0.4-200.fc29.x86_64 #1 SMP Mon Mar 25 02:27:33 UTC 2019 x86_64 x86_64 x86_64 GNU/Linux

Can you get `rpm -q container-selinux`? Also, you might want to try `setenforce 0` then running the container again, to see if it really is SELinux? Assuming it is, you might want to try a `dnf -y --enablerepo=updates-testing upgrade container-selinux` - I think the fix is in updates-testing

@rhatdan What version of container-selinux had the fix?
I ran `podman run -it postgres:9.6.9` on my F29 laptop and it ran fine.

@mheon 

I had this  version:

```
rpm -q container-selinux
container-selinux-2.91-1.gitacc6941.fc29.noarch
```

Tried to turn off SELinux and it helped. 

Then I upgraded container-selinux with your command:

```
rpm -q container-selinux                                    
container-selinux-2.95-1.gite3ebc68.fc29.noarch
```

Enabled SELinux again and tried running postgres. This time it worked. So the fix in container-selinux package works.
Ack, good to hear! I'll close, then - reopen if you have further issues
